It's my contention, so like as a copywriter I'm a lot faster, pretty much than anybody else I've ever seen or anybody I know who writes copy for a living, which is largely why I make more money at it than anybody else. It's simply because I get it done faster. But mainly it's because when I'm writing copy that's like all I'm doing. And most other copywriters that's not all they're doing, see their mind is leaving the job and coming back and leaving the job and coming back. And they're thinking about bad stuff, who died, who's about to die, who's suing, who's not suing, fight with spouse, kid, drug arrest, whatever. That's all going on as if it were 50 people in the room with them interrupting them. It's just that they're all in their head. So literally they are distracted by the voices in their head. And so probably that's a form of clinical insanity. So I like to think I'm saying everybody else is nuts, right? Because I don't hear any of those voices and you guys do. So you need help. But I mean seriously that's why I write faster is because that's all I'm thinking about. So that's an important issue. Litmus tests. Here's something you probably all have certain rules about how you do business or who you do business with or who you accept as a customer or client. You probably all have certain rules. And with varying degrees of frequency, everybody in the room has said to themselves or you get it said to you by your spouse. The second may be more often than the first because self-awareness is a rare commodity. But you've all said this to yourself. I knew I shouldn't have. I violated my own rule and now it has bit me in the ass. Right? Is there anybody who hasn't? If you haven't, it's just because you haven't really thought through and had any rules. Because otherwise you've all done it. And you know when you're doing it. It's one of the most perverse parts of human behavior. I mean, you know you're doing it to yourself. But for one reason or another, there's a financial desperation factor in play. But setting that aside, many times that's not the motivating factor. It has nothing to do with financial desperation. It's just psychosis. And I mean, you know you're doing it. You know it's going to bite you in the ass. You know you shouldn't be involving yourself one way or the other with this person. And you go ahead and do it anyway. Well, so I have no way to end that. If anybody does, I'd like to hear it. The only thing I can tell you is you can reduce it by being very, very conscious of it and making sure you know what your litmus tests and your rules are. And if necessary, have them up on the wall. If you're not able to internalize them well enough. So like I have this question and I've kept some clients from mistakes with it. For example, that is if you're about to do some kind of a business relationship with this person. Partnership, joint venture, we're going to invest in something together, whatever. Some kind of serious relationship, not a transactional thing. And then the question is, are there three people who have done some serious business relationship with him in the past who if asked would do it again? And if he can't produce three, the odds of you being the first ain't good. Not a good odds play. And there are people who, not only can't they produce three, they can't produce one. There's not a soul on the planet who's ever been in a business relationship with them who would ever have anything to do with them again. And in many cases, it's actually abundantly known. And people go ahead and, I mean, I had this conversation vehemently one time with Guthi Ranker. And that's how I finally won the argument. I said, look, I said, why you guys would want to get in bed with this guy is utterly beyond me because here's the 52 miserable, horrible things that are going to happen as a result of it. And you know at least 41 of them, but greed is over here, right? So here, at least do this. Find somebody.
